acceptance.baseline: Before this lane, resolveScreen(false, '?manifest=...') returned signin and there was no verifier-specific dev bypass.
acceptance.after: web/replay/src/devAuthBypass.ts:17-22 requires exact flag '1', loopback hostname, prod false, and mode not production. web/replay/src/AppShell.tsx:22-25 only bypasses for an explicit ?manifest= deep link; ?clip= and empty search stay signin without a token.
acceptance.target: Bypass gated on explicit env var, localhost-only request origin, and non-production mode; default off and fail-closed; production configs unaffected.

acceptance.target: Verifier can use the bypass by flag or env, and --help documents the behavior.

changes: web/replay/src/devAuthBypass.ts:17 added the fail-closed Vite client gate for REPLAY_VERIFY_DEV_BYPASS/VITE_REPLAY_VERIFY_DEV_BYPASS, loopback hostname, and non-production mode.
web/replay/src/AppShell.tsx:22 allowed unauthenticated viewer routing only for explicit manifest links when the dev verifier bypass gate is active; normal signin/library behavior remains unchanged.
scripts/racketsport/verify_process_video_viewer.py:57 added verifier-side env/flag helpers and Vite env propagation for the dev bypass.

honest_issues: Sandbox split: I unit-tested the bypass gating and verifier wiring. I did not run a live localhost/browser Playwright verify because this lane explicitly says the sandbox cannot bind localhost or launch browsers; the manager owns that post-land check.

If --dev-auth-bypass reuses an already-running Vite server, the verifier cannot retroactively inject VITE_REPLAY_VERIFY_DEV_BYPASS into that server. The verifier records this in its notes; the manager should use an unused port or start Vite with the env set.

The worktree contains unrelated dirty/protected files owned by other lanes, including scripts/racketsport/process_video.py, configs/racketsport/best_stack.json, threed/racketsport/mhr_decode.py, and existing web/replay App/viewerData edits. I did not revert or include those changes.
npm run build passed but emitted the existing Vite large-chunk warning; no build failure was present.
next: Manager post-land check: run REPLAY_VERIFY_DEV_BYPASS=1 .venv/bin/python scripts/racketsport/verify_process_video_viewer.py --manifest <run>/replay_viewer_manifest.json --out-dir runs/lanes/w7_browserbypass_20260709/live_viewer_verify --dev-auth-bypass on a browser-capable localhost environment.
